citations.text_snippet: Apple Inc. (Ticker: AAPL) | Exchange: NASDAQ | Sector: Technology | Industry: Consumer Electronics | Market Cap: N/A | CEO: Timothy D. Cook | Full-Time Employees: 164000 | IPO Date: 1980-12-12 | Country: US | Website: https://www.apple.com

paragraphs.text: Apple Inc. designs, manufactures, and markets smartphones, personal computers, tablets, wearables, and accessories worldwide. The company offers iPhone, a line of smartphones; Mac, a line of personal computers; iPad, a line of multi-purpose tablets; and wearables, home, and accessories comprising AirPods, Apple TV, Apple Watch, Beats products, and HomePod. It also provides AppleCare support and cloud services; and operates various platforms, including the App Store that allow customers to discover and download applications and digital content, such as books, music, video, games, and podcasts, as well as advertising services include third-party licensing arrangements and its own advertising platforms. In addition, the company offers various subscription-based services, such as Apple Arcade, a game subscription service; Apple Fitness+, a personalized fitness service; Apple Music, which offers users a curated listening experience with on-demand radio stations; Apple News+, a subscription news and magazine service; Apple TV+, which offers exclusive original content; Apple Card, a co-branded credit card; and Apple Pay, a cashless payment service, as well as licenses its intellectual property. The company serves consumers, and small and mid-sized businesses; and the education, enterprise, and government markets. It distributes third-party applications for its products through the App Store. The company also sells its products through its retail and online stores, and direct sales force; and third-party cellular network carriers, wholesalers, retailers, and resellers. Apple Inc. was founded in 1976 and is headquartered in Cupertino, California.

paragraphs.text: Apple's recent financial performance shows mixed signals heading into the second half of 2025. Q3 revenue declined 1.39% year-over-year to $94.04 billion, while net income fell 5.43% to $23.43 billion, reflecting margin pressure despite the company's services growth momentum. Loop Capital analysts reported Apple shipped 45 million iPhones in Q2 2025, matching forecasts, as tariff concerns and aggressive promotions helped offset an otherwise flat global smartphone market. The company maintains strong profitability metrics with Q3 gross margin at 46.49% and operating margin at 29.99%, though investor sentiment has turned cautious.

paragraphs.text: Apple Inc. operates a diversified business model centered on designing, manufacturing, and marketing consumer electronics and services worldwide. The company generates revenue through hardware products including iPhone smartphones, Mac personal computers, iPad tablets, and wearables such as AirPods, Apple Watch, and HomePod, alongside a growing services portfolio comprising the App Store, AppleCare, cloud services, and subscription offerings like Apple Music, Apple TV+, Apple Arcade, and Apple Fitness+ []. Distribution occurs through Apple's retail and online stores, direct sales force, and third-party cellular network carriers, wholesalers, and retailers []. In Q3 2025, Apple reported revenue of $94.036B with a gross margin of 46.49% and operating margin of 29.99%, reflecting the company's premium positioning and services-led margin expansion [,]. Services growth continues to drive investor interest alongside the core hardware business [].

paragraphs.text: Capital allocation risks stem from Apple's aggressive share repurchase program. The company repurchased $21.1B of stock in Q3 2025 alone, following $25.9B in Q2 2025. While buybacks return capital to shareholders, this substantial cash outflow ($47B over two quarters) occurs alongside declining revenue and elevated debt levels. This capital allocation strategy could limit financial flexibility for strategic investments, particularly in AI infrastructure and technology development where competitors are making significant commitments. The combination of high buyback activity, negative revenue growth, and a weak current ratio suggests potential tension between shareholder returns and balance sheet strengthening.

evidence_appendix.text: Apple Inc. (Ticker: AAPL) | Exchange: NASDAQ | Sector: Technology | Industry: Consumer Electronics | Market Cap: N/A | CEO: Timothy D. Cook | Full-Time Employees: 164000 | IPO Date: 1980-12-12 | Country: US | Website: https://www.apple.com

Apple Inc. designs, manufactures, and markets smartphones, personal computers, tablets, wearables, and accessories worldwide. The company offers iPhone, a line of smartphones; Mac, a line of personal computers; iPad, a line of multi-purpose tablets; and wearables, home, and accessories comprising AirPods, Apple TV, Apple Watch, Beats products, and HomePod. It also provides AppleCare support and cloud services; and operates various platforms, including the App Store that allow customers to discover and download applications and digital content, such as books, music, video, games, and podcasts, as well as advertising services include third-party licensing arrangements and its own advertising platforms. In addition, the company offers various subscription-based services, such as Apple Arcade, a game subscription service; Apple Fitness+, a personalized fitness service; Apple Music, which offers users a curated listening experience with on-demand radio stations; Apple News+, a subscription news and magazine service; Apple TV+, which offers exclusive original content; Apple Card, a co-branded credit card; and Apple Pay, a cashless payment service, as well as licenses its intellectual property. The company serves consumers, and small and mid-sized businesses; and the education, enterprise, and government markets. It distributes third-party applications for its products through the App Store. The company also sells its products through its retail and online stores, and direct sales force; and third-party cellular network carriers, wholesalers, retailers, and resellers. Apple Inc. was founded in 1976 and is headquartered in Cupertino, California.

Title: Should You Sell Apple's Stock Before July 31?
URL: https://finnhub.io/api/news?id=562108f76534fd602841e9346ffa66a55556185f316840d982def8fb8794e0a7
Apple has fallen behind in the AI arms race.  This could apply to Apple (NASDAQ: AAPL), as investors have been fairly bearish on the stock for a good reason.  While the rest of the market has rallied from its lows in April and May, Apple's stock has remained relatively flat, down 15% for the year at the time of this writing.

[NEWS] 2025-07-18T19:00:52Z | Yahoo
Title: Apple races ahead in epic $150M F1 media rights bid
URL: https://finnhub.io/api/news?id=13b692020babfda05e8fb026d10a053bfa0743732342ac8fd317d099107b11dd
Apple (AAPL) is putting their pedal to the metal in their race for F1 media rights. This week on Yahoo Finance Sports Report, host Joe Pompliano takes a look at some of this week’s biggest headlines in the sports business world that you and your portfolio need to know. From Apple’s $150 million bid for Formula 1 media rights to FanDuel’s $31 billion valuation, sports business is being transformed by big financial plays. Yahoo Sports Senior Writer Frank Schwab joins the show to talk about NFL training camp and what to expect for the league heading into the 2025-26 season. Plus, Learfield CEO Cole Gahagan stops by to discuss college sports marketing and how Learfield is helping schools build revenue in a world of NIL, stadium naming rights, athletic fees, and more. Yahoo Finance Sports Report with Joe Pompliano, a vodcast brought to you by Yahoo Finance and Yahoo Sports, looks beyond the latest sports business headlines, analyzes all the need-to-know news - the teams, trades, and billion dollar deals - so you and your portfolio will win BIG. Yahoo Finance Sports Report is developed and produced by Lauren Pokedoff.

metadata.title: Apple races ahead in epic $150M F1 media rights bid
